message:
test(ft-xbnl0.2.4): request-target path+query roundtrip contract (tick 321)

Added `distributed_http_client_transmits_full_request_target` — pins
that `DistributedHttpClient::get` transmits the full URL request-target
to the server: path segments AND query string, without dropping,
reordering, or double-encoding.

Why this matters: the HTTP client is used by distributed event
forwarding and health checks; both rely on query-string parameters
(`?src=...`, `?agent_id=...`) and nested paths (`/api/v1/events`)
arriving at the server exactly as constructed. A regression in URL
parsing that dropped the query string would silently degrade event
routing in production without surfacing a transport error.

Test sends GET against
`http://127.0.0.1:<port>/api/v1/events?src=rustic-maple&seq=42` and
asserts the server-received request line starts with
`GET /api/v1/events?src=rustic-maple&seq=42 HTTP/1.` (the trailing
version-prefix check tolerates HTTP/1.0 vs HTTP/1.1 without pinning a
specific minor version).

Test result: ok. 1 passed; 0 failed
Compile time: 1m 17s cold.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
